title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe: Authentic Bo Lo Bao at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn to bake the perfect Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Enjoy fluffy, sweet bread with a crispy, crumbly topping. Get the authentic taste of Hong Kong at home!
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?
Step-by-Step Guide to Baking Bo Lo Bao
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Topping
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden, crispy topping, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the sweet, fluffy goodness of homemade Pineapple Buns, a classic Hong Kong bakery treat!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery staple, famous for their sweet, crumbly, golden topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Beneath this delightful crust lies a soft, fluffy bread roll, creating a perfect balance of textures and flavors. This recipe guides you to recreate this iconic treat, bringing the authentic taste of a Chinese bakery right into your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 300g flour, 50g sugar, yeast, and salt. Add the beaten egg and warm milk. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened 30g butter and knead for another 5 minutes until fully absorbed and the dough is soft and pliable. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let it proof in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Topping Crust
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the pineapple topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 80g softened butter and 80g sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until well combined. Sift in 120g flour, milk powder, and baking powder. Mix until a soft, crumbly dough forms. Do not overmix. Cover the topping dough and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, divide the chilled topping dough into 8 equal portions. Flatten each topping portion into a thin disc, large enough to cover about two-thirds of a bread bun. Carefully place a topping disc over each dough ball, pressing gently to adhere.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Preheat Oven
instructions.text: Place the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof again in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Towards the end of the second proofing, preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F).

instructions.title: Egg Wash and Bake
instructions.text: Just before baking, gently brush the exposed bread dough (not the topping) with the beaten egg wash. You can also lightly brush the topping for extra shine. Bake the pineapple buns in the preheated oven for 18-22 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the pineapple buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm for the best taste and texture. Pineapple buns are traditionally enjoyed plain, with a slab of butter, or alongside a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea.

tips: Do not overmix the topping dough; a crumbly texture is desired for that signature crispy crust. Overmixing can make it tough.
For an extra rich flavor, substitute a small portion of the milk powder in the topping with custard powder.
For the crispiest topping, ensure your oven is fully preheated before baking and avoid opening the oven door frequently during the first 10 minutes.
article: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a beloved Chinese Baking dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
